sourcePattern.name: Teaching agents product design at Vercel
adaptedPrinciples: accepted agent decisions live in the repository and are reviewed like code
a short entrypoint routes to focused references instead of duplicating guidance
request mode is resolved before acting so reviews, proof, and implementation stay separate
stable rules cite canonical sources, examples, exceptions, and coverage gaps
mechanical checks belong in deterministic lint/eval fixtures; judgment stays in guidance with evidence
evidence write-back stores pending retro packets under nucleus/retro/ for human PR review before guidance changes

model.skills: routing, playbooks, triggers, guardrails, references, exemplars, and stable rules
model.agents: delegated specialists executing bounded work packets selected by mode, source routing, and lens
model.harnessAdapters: format translators only; they must preserve canonical names, behavior contracts, routing, and output packets
model.deterministicChecks: lint/eval fixtures enforce reliable mechanical rules and trigger/application behavior
model.humanGovernance: new standards and product/workflow policy require human acceptance before becoming canonical guidance

repositoryStructure.rootInstruction: AGENTS.md tells coding agents when to load the relevant nucleus agent skill package.
repositoryStructure.mappingDecision: Vercel documents one repo-local product-design skill. Loom adapts that shape per canonical nucleus agent: every agent gets its own Vercel-shaped skill folder with lens references instead of one giant shared super-skill or one agent per behavioral variant.

perAgentSkillPackage.agentGovernance: AGENTS.md defines load order, validation, ownership, and update rules for that specific agent package.
perAgentSkillPackage.exemplars: exemplars/pr-{name}.md records accepted decisions worth repeating and mistakes to avoid.
perAgentSkillPackage.coverageGaps: references/coverage-gaps.md keeps missing standards visible instead of letting agents invent policy.
perAgentSkillPackage.lensReferences: references/lens-{lens-name}.md carries variant guidance loaded only when the packet names that lens.

repositoryStructure.utilitySkills: nucleus/utilities/{skill-name}/ holds the four repo-owned kit utilities (assembler, prospect, space-age, map-seed). Cited engines moved operator-local per docs/skills/operator-local-manifest.md.
repositoryStructure.evalFixtures: tooling/scripts/shared-agent-eval/ tests trigger loading separately from rule application and holdout behavior.

requestModes.typicalRequest: design the workflow, define agent model, compare alternatives
requestModes.requiredBehavior: frame the job, evidence, alternatives, acceptance criteria, risks, non-goals, and open decisions; do not edit or activate agents unless asked

requestModes.typicalRequest: build, wire, render, or update a scoped agent feature
requestModes.requiredBehavior: load routed references, implement the smallest coherent change, and keep within one issue/branch/PR

requestModes.typicalRequest: audit, critique, review PR, find risks
requestModes.requiredBehavior: inspect source and proof, report prioritized findings with source/rule citations, and do not edit unless asked

requestModes.typicalRequest: verify, smoke test, gather evidence, check behavior
requestModes.requiredBehavior: run proof-only validation, cite what was exercised, and avoid feature expansion

requestModes.typicalRequest: fix this finding, address one review comment
requestModes.requiredBehavior: consume a finding packet, fix exactly that scope, rerun the named proof, and return changed files plus residual risk
enforce launch gates and tracker bridge; no child agent may merge, close issues, or apply to live HOME unless the launch gate owns that permission

lensPolicy.selection: The input packet may name one or more lenses; when lens is absent, the agent loads its mode-default lens reference.
lensPolicy.loading: Only the named lens references load; unnamed lens references stay unloaded to keep context small.
lensPolicy.boundaries: Lenses select guidance only; they never widen packet scope, change the request-mode boundary, or grant extra delegation authority.
lensPolicy.fanout: Review and proof coordinators may fan out one child per lens in parallel when each lens has a distinct finding contract.

loadOrder: coverage gaps before inventing new standards
skipSurfaces: backend-only work with no agent/workflow/user-visible effect
generated files unless the task is rendering those files
documentation-only copy edits outside the active agent contract
decisionAuthority: verified repo code/tests and live PR state
routing.reportingRequirement: Agents report mode, lens, target surface, loaded references, rule IDs, proof run, and unresolved coverage gaps.

useLinterWhen: code can identify the failure without rendering
the rule avoids likely false positives
the violation has a concrete fix
useAgentGuidanceWhen: the decision needs product, workflow, or codebase context
the rule would need many exceptions
the standard is new or policy-bearing
evals: holdout fixtures without expected edits in guidance

evidenceIntake.purpose: capture source-grounded launch retros as pending review packets; accepted guidance still requires human PR approval before merge
evidenceIntake.homeRationale: retro packets are evidence under nucleus but are not accepted skill guidance; this pending home avoids editing rule, exemplar, or coverage-gap destinations until human review chooses the narrow target
guidanceChangePolicy: retro packets stay pending until a human reviews the generated PR
accepted rules must satisfy the enforced ruleSchema
accepted exemplars, rules, and coverage gaps land in the narrowest relevant nucleus/skills/{agent-name}/ destination
the retro generator never merges PRs, edits accepted guidance directly, or applies changes to live HOME
acceptedChangeRequirements: target file chosen by narrowest relevant destination
evidenceIntake.humanReviewGate: the retro PR is the HITL gate; reviewers accept, redirect, defer, or reject each pending packet

aspirationalDesign.summary: collector, judge, decision-log, and destination automation remain design vocabulary only; no validator or tool treats that machine as live unless a future issue implements it

delegationPolicy.depthModel: Root issue/PR owner is depth 0; each autonomous child wave increments depth by one.
delegationPolicy.parentIntegrationOwnership: The parent that spawned a wave owns integration, conflict resolution, final proof selection, and tracker/PR reporting.
hardStops: no allowed child for the current mode and scope
requested action would widen beyond the parent packet
coverage gap blocks a rule, standard, or source needed for the next step
proof is red or missing before launch
native agent rendering, live HOME apply, PR merge, or issue closeout is requested in this contract slice
forbiddenAutonomousActions: apply generated files to live HOME
create native OMP, Codex, or Claude agent files in this contract slice
invent new standards when references or rules are missing

delegationModes.advanceRule: Advance only from an accepted plan, issue decomposition, or documented coverage-gap route.
Advance through disjoint implementation scopes, then lens-fanned proof/review waves, then repair loops owned by the parent.
Advance only by returning prioritized findings or a no-finding statement to the parent; distinct lenses may run as parallel biters children.
Advance only by returning observed evidence, artifacts, and untested branches; distinct proof lenses may run as parallel lab children.

Advance one concrete finding to one repair packet, then to repair-pack or lab for the named proof only; the coordinator reruns the minimal-diff lens after the diff changes, not as repair-pack delegation.
Rocket-launch records launch-gate evidence for the tracker bridge; lab and belt may only provide evidence.

workflowTransitions.recordedBy: parent orchestrator before and after every autonomous wave

parallelFanout.writeScopes: Implementation children may run in parallel only when each packet names disjoint files or one parent owns all integration edits.
parallelFanout.reviewScopes: Review/proof children may inspect the same diff in parallel when each lens has a distinct finding contract.
parallelFanout.coordination: Siblings coordinate through the parent packet or explicit messages; they do not spawn a second unmanaged swarm.

sequence: implement scoped issue work in one branch/worktree
fan out lab and biters across proof and review lenses (correctness, security, ui-proof, drift)
run the biters minimal-diff lens after the first proof/review wave
send one concrete finding at a time to repair-pack
rerun the named proof through lab or the parent
return review-ready PR packet to the parent; rocket-launch records launch-gate evidence while the tracker bridge owns closeout outside this contract slice
roboportsDAG.loopBack: repair-pack returns to lab and the parent; repeated findings require fresh compact packets.

packageShape.exemplars: exemplars/finding-{stable-id}.md records accepted one-finding fixes, proof reruns, and rejected drive-by cleanup.

optionalDelegatedModes.condition: only for the named proof check in the finding packet

findingPacketSchema.symbol: Function, object, section, or contract field implicated by the finding.
findingPacketSchema.scope: Smallest behavior or document boundary the fix may touch.
findingPacketSchema.concreteRisk: Observed failure mode or review risk; no speculative cleanup.
findingPacketSchema.minimalExpectedFix: Smallest acceptable correction; not a redesign.
findingPacketSchema.proofCheck: Named command, test, or scenario that must be rerun after the fix.
findingPacketSchema.ruleOrSourceId: Stable rule id, issue acceptance criterion, review id, or source link that justifies the finding.
findingPacketSchema.nonGoals: Explicit exclusions that prevent adjacent fixes.
findingPacketSchema.allowedFiles: Closed list of files repair-pack may edit; parent must reissue the packet if the list is wrong.

rules: Accept exactly one concrete finding per packet.
Start from fresh compact context: issue/PR id, finding packet, relevant excerpts, allowed files, and named proof only.
Refuse drive-by cleanup, style-only edits, broad refactors, or acceptance-criteria changes.
Do not spawn broad workflow agents or delegate review back through the full DAG.
Stop on missing allowed files, missing proof check, missing source/rule id, or a required fix outside packet scope.
Return changed files, proof result, residual risk, and blocker reason when blocked.

delegation.condition: lab may run only the named proof check from the finding packet after the fix or to confirm a blocked proof state

postFixLoop: repair-pack applies the minimal fix inside allowed files
lab reruns the named proof when delegated or the coordinator reruns it directly
the coordinator reruns the biters minimal-diff lens only when the diff changed or the fix risks scope creep
coordinator integrates the repaired diff, updates the finding state, and decides whether another fresh packet is needed

originalImplementerPolicy.avoidByDefault: Do not consult the original implementer for routine fixes; fresh context reduces anchoring, context drag, and defense of the original design.
consultWhen: the finding depends on undocumented intent that cannot be recovered from issue, code, or proof evidence
the minimal fix would alter an API, data contract, migration, or user-visible workflow the implementer owns
the allowed files are insufficient and the coordinator needs scope clarification
originalImplementerPolicy.consultHow: Ask one narrow question through the coordinator, then return to the packet; do not invite the implementer to re-own the repair.

coverageGapPolicy.defaultAction: stop or route instead of inventing new standards
routes: record the missing standard in references/coverage-gaps.md
return blocker to parent when the issue acceptance criteria require human policy
propose deterministic checks only when the linter-vs-guidance rule passes

agents.name: blueprint
agents.purpose: Owns shape work: turns current context into a PRD/spec with acceptance criteria, non-goals, proof plan, and open decisions, and covers issue decomposition, architecture seams, research spikes, and tracker triage through lenses.
nonGoals: Do not live-apply to real HOME
Do not stamp issues or plans from thin context

agents.name: roboports
agents.purpose: Runs one tracked issue through branch/worktree, implementation, proof, review, and PR readiness, and covers behavior-preserving refactors and measured performance work through lenses.
nonGoals: Do not live-apply to real HOME
Do not widen beyond the issue

agents.name: biters
agents.purpose: Attacks the change before merge across correctness, security, minimal-diff, and workflow-drift lenses, reporting prioritized findings without editing.
nonGoals: Do not live-apply to real HOME

agents.name: lab
agents.purpose: Runs proof-only validation and records behavior evidence without expanding scope, covering command, UI, and smoke proof through lenses.
nonGoals: Do not live-apply to real HOME

agents.name: repair-pack
agents.purpose: Fixes exactly one concrete review/proof finding from a fresh compact packet.
nonGoals: Do not live-apply to real HOME
Do not implement native agent files or eval harnesses

agents.name: rocket-launch
agents.purpose: Records launch-gate readiness only after review/proof/CI gates and tracker bridge evidence are satisfied.
nonGoals: Do not merge PRs in this contract slice
Do not close issues by hand to fake bridge closeout
Do not live-apply to real HOME
Do not widen scope at launch

agents.name: belt
agents.purpose: Moves durable context between agents/threads with concise state, proof, risks, and next actions, covering handoff, thread-control, and resume flows through lenses.
nonGoals: Do not include transcripts by default
Do not live-apply to real HOME

supersededCandidates.reason: Direct OMP designer role port; UI workflow proof belongs to the lab ui-proof lens and routed references, not an omp-prefixed agent.
Direct OMP planning role port; planning work belongs to blueprint lenses and roboports.
Direct OMP reviewer role port; review work belongs to biters lenses, lab, and stable rule citations.
Potential OMP research role port; research belongs to the blueprint research-spike lens and source-grounded references.

activation.status: plugin-bridge activation machinery is retired; packages load directly from the canonical repo tree
activation.nativeAgentRendering: not planned; shared packages ship as portable skills, not direct OMP/Codex/Claude role-agent files
activation.packageSource: canonical repo-local Vercel-shaped package source under nucleus/skills/{agent-name}/
activation.evidenceDecisionOwner: pending retro packet -> human PR review -> accepted guidance only after approval
activation.evidenceIntakeAutomation: practiced core only: scripts/retro-packet.mjs writes pending nucleus/retro packets; collector/judge/destination automation is aspirational
localOnlyRuntimeData: OMP auth, sessions, histories, caches, DBs, terminal state, logs, and local settings
Codex auth, sessions, histories, caches, DBs, browser state, blobs, plugin caches, and local settings
Claude auth, sessions, histories, caches, DBs, daemon state, plugin caches/data, and local settings
activation.liveHomeApply: requires explicit HITL approval; forbidden by default

commonInvariants: report mode, lens, target surface, loaded references, rule IDs, proof run, and unresolved coverage gaps
no scope widening beyond the packet
no issue closeout or PR merge in this contract slice; launch records gates for the tracker bridge
no native harness agent rendering in this contract slice

context.validation: Exercised against fixtures or dry-run surfaces; report intended tracker, PR, and live-HOME side effects instead of performing them.
context.live: Normal operation within the mode boundary.
context.absentBehavior: Assume live and state the assumption in the output packet.
